section: DESIGN OF NEW ATTENUATION TESTING FACILITIES
text: Specifically, the new facility should be capable of measuring attenuations of some $10^{8}$ in fast neutrons, should have a variable intensity source and should permit measurements through various shapes of shields (i.e., cylindrical or possibly spherical in addition to flat plates).

A fifteen-hour exposure at full power reduced the resistance from 48,300 to 234 ohms.

A similar resistor in a chamber evacuated to approximately nine microns showed a resistance change of from 51,000 to 252 ohms within two minutes after 44 centimeters of hydrogen was applied to the system.
